sections.location: Hallway outside each bedroom (2nd floor), kitchen ceiling near the stove, and basement near the utility room.
tips: Test smoke detectors at least once a month using the test button.
Replace batteries regularly or when the detector chirps. Change your clocks, change your batteries!
Vacuum or dust detectors regularly and follow manufacturer instructions.
Never borrow a battery from a smoke detector.
Make sure everyone in the home can hear and recognize the alarm sound.

sections.location: Located in the utility closet on the main floor, behind the door to the left of the garage entrance.
sections.notes: The water heater is set to 120°F. Do not adjust the temperature above 140°F. If you notice a lack of hot water or unusual odors, please contact us immediately.

sections.location: In the garage on the far wall (right side), mounted at eye level in a gray metal panel box. Each breaker is labeled.
tips: If a breaker trips, switch it fully OFF then back ON to reset.
If breakers keep tripping, contact us — do not repeatedly reset.

sections.location: Main water shut off is in the basement, on the front wall near the water meter. Turn the handle clockwise (right) to shut off water.
sections.notes: In the event of a burst pipe or major leak, shut off the water immediately and contact us. Individual shut-off valves are also located under each sink and behind each toilet.

sections.location: Return air vent in the hallway ceiling of the 1st floor. Filter size: 16×25×1.
tips: Replace the filter every 60–90 days, or more often if you have pets.
A clogged filter reduces efficiency and may damage the unit.
Replacement filters are available at most hardware stores.

sections.location: Exterior of the house on the right side, mounted to the brick wall near the back corner. Account number is on the meter face.
sections.notes: Utility service is set up in the tenant's name. Contact Duke Energy to establish your account before move-in.

sections.location: Front door (doorbell cam), rear patio (soffit-mounted), and driveway (left eave). All cameras are exterior only.
sections.notes: Cameras are managed by the property owner for exterior security only. No interior cameras exist. Footage is retained for 30 days. Contact us for access or security concerns.
